issues.item: an-suffix-nominalization cloze_mcq #1 (options Makanan/Makan), cloze_mcq #2 (kiriman/kirim), cloze_mcq #3 (minuman/minum); contrast_pair #1 (makanan/makan), #2 (kiriman/kirim), #3 (pikiran/pikir)
issues.issue: Linter flagged 11 cloze_mcq/contrast_pair option arrays where one option is a whole-word prefix of another (root vs -AN derivation: makan/makanan, kirim/kiriman, minum/minuman; ordinal: dua/kedua, satu/kesatu; conjunction: karena/karena itu). The root-vs-derived contrast IS the pedagogical point, so the CONTENT is correct, but the mechanical substring-duplicate rule fires and the publish gate (publish-approved-content.ts:40-48) treats any lint CRITICAL as a hard exit-non-zero block. VERDICT: these candidates must be regenerated with non-substring distractors OR the affix/ordinal contrast must be moved exclusively into contrast_pair/sentence_transformation/constrained_translation forms (which are not subject to the 4-option substring rule the same way). As wired today, they block publish.
issues.fix: For each flagged cloze_mcq, replace the substring-overlapping distractor with a non-overlapping content word of the same class from the cumulative pool that is still a plausible Dutch-speaker error (e.g. for the makanan blank keep Minuman + Pikiran and swap the bare-stem Makan distractor for an unrelated noun like Sayuran or Pesanan). Keep the root-vs-derived teaching contrast in the contrast_pair candidates, which the linter's substring rule still flags — so for those, confirm with the orchestrator whether the substring rule should special-case morphological minimal pairs; otherwise rephrase the two options so neither is a literal whole-word prefix of the other (not feasible for a true minimal pair, so regeneration to a different exercise type or a linter carve-out is required).

issues.item: dialogue_chunk items (13 lines), translation_nl is empty string
issues.issue: All 13 dialogue_chunk items carry translation_nl: "". The task notes this is propagated from lesson.ts dialogue lines at publish (propagateDialogueTranslations.ts, Stage B). HOWEVER the lint gate in publish-approved-content.ts runs BEFORE Stage B (lines 40-48), and lint flags the empty translation as CRITICAL, exiting non-zero — so propagation never gets the chance to run. As the pipeline is wired today this is a true pre-publish blocker, not a benign deferred fill.
issues.fix: Populate translation_nl on each dialogue_chunk in learning-items.ts directly from the matching lesson.ts dialogue line translations (they are all present and correct in lesson.ts lines 31-94). This makes lint pass; the Stage B propagation then becomes a no-op/confirmation.

issues.item: dialogue cloze for the long directions line, cloze_answer 'becak'
issues.issue: The blanked token normalises to 'becak' but the only matching learning_item is base_text 'becak (bècak)' (normalized_text 'becak (bècak)'). The blank word must resolve to a learning_item normalized_text in the current or a prior lesson; 'becak' (without the parenthetical pronunciation gloss) does not match, so the runtime distractor cascade has no anchor item. Confirmed: SELECT on learning_items shows no row with normalized_text 'becak'.
issues.fix: Either (a) change the item base_text in learning-items.ts to a clean 'becak' and move the '(bècak)' pronunciation gloss to a pronunciation/notes field so the cloze answer matches, or (b) re-target this dialogue cloze to a different token whose normalized form is in the pool (e.g. blank 'jembatan' or 'kiri'). Option (a) is preferred because the same gloss-in-base_text pattern affects belok (bèlok), paket (pakèt), rokok kretek (krètèk) — any dialogue/vocab cloze whose answer is one of those will hit the same mismatch.

issues.issue: 'lho!' is already declared in lesson-7/learning-items.ts AND published to the DB (confirmed: indonesian.learning_items has base_text 'lho!', pos 'particle'). findDuplicateItems (capability-stage/lint/duplicateItems.ts) emits a CRITICAL duplicate-item-cross-lesson finding because two lessons declare the same itemSlug; Decision 3b requires exactly one introducing lesson per capability. The publish gate blocks on this.
issues.fix: Remove 'lho!' from lesson-10/learning-items.ts. If lesson 10's dialogue/material needs to surface it, reference it via lesson-page-blocks.ts source_refs[] (the M:N exposure bridge) pointing at the lesson-7 item. Also remove its cloze-contexts.ts entry and vocab-enrichments.ts entry, since the item will no longer be introduced here.

issues.item: Multiple distractors not in the cumulative pool
issues.issue: Several distractors are not present in indonesian.learning_items nor in the L1-L10 cumulative pool: 'sepi' (aman cloze; ramai cloze), 'manis' (asin cloze), 'bangkit' (bangun cued_recall), 'terakhir' (pertama cloze), 'selalu' (kadang-kadang cloze), 'terkenal' (termasyhur cued_recall), 'ramah' (ramai cued_recall), 'rindang' (rindu cued_recall), 'musim' (museum cued_recall), 'mampu' (mampir cued_recall), 'mesin' (Mesir cued_recall), 'universal' (universitas cued_recall), 'ujian' (ujung cued_recall), 'pandang' (pandai cued_recall), 'penyebrang' (penyeberangan cued_recall), 'pertanyaan' (pertama cued_recall), 'kebersihan'/'kesehatan' (kesenian cued_recall), 'jembrana' (jembatan cued_recall), 'termasuk' (termasyhur cued_recall), 'sehat'. These appear in MCQ distractor slots; runtime renders them as wrong options. Per §12 this is WARNING (does not block publish). Many are deliberately near-miss confusables (mesin~Mesir, musim~museum, ujian~ujung) which is good distractor craft, but the learner will see Indonesian words never taught. Admin should review whether near-miss-by-form distractors outside the pool are acceptable for this lesson.
issues.fix: Where the intent is a same-meaning confusable, prefer an in-pool word (e.g. for 'aman' use in-pool 'kosong'/'bersih' rather than 'sepi'). Where the intent is a deliberate orthographic near-miss (mesin/musim/ujian), keep only if the admin accepts out-of-pool distractors; otherwise replace with in-pool members of the same word class.

issues.item: rasa-kira-pikir-mental-verbs constrained_translation #5 (sourceLanguageSentence 'Ik denk dat het ongeveer tien kilometer is')
issues.issue: acceptableAnswers[0] 'Saya kira kira-kira sepuluh kilometer.' stacks 'kira' (mental verb) directly with 'kira-kira' (approximately) — grammatical but stylistically awkward/repetitive; a learner producing 'Saya kira sekitar sepuluh kilometer' is more natural. 'sekitar' is not in the cumulative pool though, so the natural variant relies on out-of-pool vocab. Minor; admin may prefer to swap the source sentence to avoid the kira/kira-kira clash.
issues.fix: Consider changing the source sentence to avoid the double-kira, e.g. 'Ik denk dat de batik fijner is' (reuses in-pool halus) so the answer is a clean single mental-verb construction.

issues.item: rasa-kira-pikir-mental-verbs contrast_pair #3 (Jangan pikir-pikir vs Jangan rasa-rasa)
issues.issue: The distractor 'Jangan rasa-rasa tentang hal itu' is, as the explanationText itself admits, an 'ongebruikelijke' (non-existent/ungrammatical) form. A reduplicated 'rasa-rasa' in this slot is not a real Dutch-speaker error the way a wrong-but-attested word would be; it is closer to an obviously-wrong option. The contrast still teaches pikir vs rasa, but the wrong option is not a genuine attested competitor. Non-blocking.
issues.fix: Consider a distractor that is an attested but wrong choice, e.g. 'Jangan kira-kira tentang hal itu' (kira-kira is a real lesson item), which forces the pikir-vs-kira discrimination rather than offering a non-word.

issues.issue: subordinating-conjunctions is the most confusable pattern in the lesson (the description and key_contrast both name supaya/agar-vs-sehingga and karena-vs-karena-itu as the core learner confusions) yet confusion_group is null. The other six patterns all carry a confusion_group. Setting one would let the runtime cluster confusable conjunction exercises.
issues.fix: Set confusion_group to e.g. 'conjunctions' or 'subordinators' on this pattern.

checks.lesson_ts_structure: PASS -- all 4 grammar sections use categories[] with rules/table/examples; exercises section uses sections[] (6 Oefeningen); 'Denken en voelen' is a text section (not grammar) which is acceptable. All translation/grammar_drill items (Oefening I-VI) have answer populated.
checks.grammar_patterns_fields: PASS -- all 7 patterns have pattern_name, description, slug (kebab-case), complexity_score in 1-10. Complexity scores (3-6) match actual complexity; rasa-kira-pikir at 6 and an-suffix at 5 are appropriately the hardest.
checks.slug_uniqueness_staging: PASS -- all 7 slugs (an-suffix-nominalization, ke-ordinal-numbers, arithmetic-operators, subordinating-conjunctions, direction-route-instructions, bare-imperative-and-invitation, rasa-kira-pikir-mental-verbs) are absent from lessons 1-9 staging files; the header comment confirms the verification.
checks.slug_uniqueness_db: PASS -- queried SELECT slug FROM indonesian.grammar_patterns (47 rows); none of the 7 lesson-10 slugs collide.
checks.candidate_payloads: PASS (structural) -- every candidate nests content under payload; all required fields present per type; cloze_mcq has exactly 4 options and a correctOptionId present in options; contrast_pair has exactly 2 options with id == text (Indonesian word/phrase, never a/b); sentence_transformation and constrained_translation have non-empty acceptableAnswers and a requiredTargetPattern matching a lesson slug; no speaking candidates. correctOptionId matches an option in every case checked.
checks.contrast_pair_option_ids: PASS -- every contrast_pair option.id equals option.text and correctOptionId matches one of them.
checks.exercise_type_coverage: PASS -- every one of the 7 patterns has >=1 of each of cloze_mcq, contrast_pair, sentence_transformation, constrained_translation, and >=12 total candidates (each has ~14-15). Production-heavy split (4 sent-transform, 5 constrained-translation) per pattern is on target.
checks.dialogue_cloze_quality: FAIL -- 9 dialogue clozes; 8 are well-formed with unique-fit blanks (pos, ramai, ingat, terletak, prangko, Pandai, rindu, mampir), but the 'becak' blank in the long directions line fails blank-not-in-pool (cloze_answer 'becak' vs item 'becak (bècak)') -- see CRITICAL above. Semantic uniqueness (13d) otherwise holds: each blanked token is the unique natural fit given the surrounding line.
checks.cloze_skips_validity: PASS -- all 4 clozeSkips ('Pak! Pak!', 'Ya, ada apa?', 'Awas kenapa, Pak?', 'O ya, itu saran baik!') carry reason 'below_6_token_threshold' and each is indeed under 6 whitespace tokens. 13 dialogue chunks = 9 authored clozes + 4 skips, exact partition.
checks.cloze_context_coverage: PASS -- every word/phrase vocab item has >=1 cloze whose learning_item_slug matches its normalized_text, including museum and kucing (vocab-list-only, not in dialogue). 'lho!' has one cloze (discourse particle, exempt but provided) -- moot once 'lho!' is removed per the duplicate-item CRITICAL.
checks.pattern_brief_integrity: PASS -- all 7 brief slugs match grammar-patterns.ts; vocabulary_pool is non-empty and every entry has item_type; research_notes present for all 7; each pattern has 4 example_sentences (>=3).
checks.vocab_enrichments_coverage: PASS (coverage) / WARNING (distractor pool) -- all 66 learning items have an entry; each has all 3 distractor arrays of exactly 3 items; no distractor equals its correct answer; no within-array duplicates found. WARNING: ~20 distractors are out-of-pool (see distractor-not-in-pool WARNING). cued_recall arrays do not contain morphological variants of the answer.
